Operation 'sendSms' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Current description is too minimal. data.current_description: Send a text message to the provided phone number
data.description_suggestion: Sends an SMS message to one or more phone numbers. Accepts message content, recipient phone number(s), and optional parameters such as sender ID, scheduled delivery time, and delivery reports. Returns a message ID (positive integer) on success or an error code (negative integer) on failure. Consumes SMS credits from the account based on message length and destination country. Supports up to 190+ countries.
data.reasoning: Current description is too minimal. Missing critical details about: what parameters are accepted (12 parameters available), what the response contains (message ID vs error code), side effects (credit consumption), and scope (multi-country support). Enhanced description provides AI agents with clear understanding of inputs, outputs, and state changes.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the delivery status of a previously sent SMS message. Requires the message ID returned from the sendSms operation. Returns the current status (e.g., pending, delivered, failed, rejected) and may include delivery timestamp and error details if applicable. No credits are consumed by this operation.
data.reasoning: Description is completely missing. data.description_suggestion: Retrieves comprehensive details for a specific SMS message including delivery status, recipient information, message content metadata, cost, and delivery timestamps. Requires the message ID as input. Returns a MessageDetails object containing all message attributes. This is a read-only operation with no side effects.

message: Operation 'cancelMessage' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description is missing. Added critical details about: preconditions (message must be scheduled/pending), required input (message ID), possible outcomes (success vs error scenarios), and side effects (potential credit refunds). This helps AI agents understand when this operation is applicable and what state changes occur.

data.description_suggestion: Cancels a scheduled SMS message that has not yet been sent. Requires the message ID of the scheduled message. Only messages with pending status can be cancelled. Returns a success confirmation or error code if cancellation fails (e.g., message already sent or not found). Successfully cancelled messages may result in credit refunds depending on account policies.
data.reasoning: Description is missing. Added critical details about: preconditions (message must be scheduled/pending), required input (message ID), possible outcomes (success vs error scenarios), and side effects (potential credit refunds). data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current SMS credit balance for the authenticated account. Returns the available credits as a numeric value. Credits are consumed when sending SMS messages and may be refunded for cancelled messages. This is a read-only operation with no side effects.

data.current_summary: Get SMS pricing by country and operator
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves SMS pricing information for specified countries and/or operators. Accepts optional country code and operator parameters to filter results. Returns a PricingResponse array containing per-message costs for each country/operator combination. Pricing varies based on destination and operator. This is a read-only operation useful for cost estimation before sending messages.

data.description_suggestion: Verifies a PIN code received via SMS, typically used for two-factor authentication or account verification workflows. Requires the PIN code, message ID (or session ID), and verification parameters. Returns a success confirmation (positive response) or error code (negative response) indicating whether the PIN is valid and not expired. This operation is commonly used after sending a verification SMS to confirm user identity.
